meta.title: Catastrophic Hemorrhage
notes: Venous hemorrhage: blood flows out (can be alarming). Arterial hemorrhage: blood spurts out in time with the heartbeat.
At this stage, the venous bleeding must be controlled.
Record the time (24:00 format) when the tourniquet was applied: on the tourniquet and on the PCR.

Tourniquet during Mass Casualty Incident: record application on the triage chart; if possible the patient should be supervised by a witness (to prevent the tourniquet from being removed); depending on circumstances (unconsciousness, no witness, etc.) note a distinguishing mark on the patient's forehead.

hem_01.text: Control of external bleeding: direct compression on the wound; compression dressing; if available, hemostatic compression dressing.

hem_03.text: Second compression bandage on top of the first.

hem_05.text: Installation of tourniquet. (Position: one palm's width above the wound, between the heart and the wound; not on the joints. Effectiveness: stop of bleeding. This is a very painful procedure; ensure the patient does not remove the tourniquet.)

hem_07.text: Urgent and safe transport to a health center.
